data.title: Creality 3D Shareholders Clear All Resolutions at 2026 AGM
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Shenzhen Creality 3D Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3388) ) has issued an update.

Shenzhen Creality 3D Technology Co., Ltd. reported that its annual general meeting held on June 30, 2026, in Hong Kong was attended by most board members and the controlling shareholders, who together represent about 67.49% of the company’s issued H share capital. All attending shareholders, whether present in person or via electronic means, were counted in the official poll, underscoring strong participation in the company’s governance.

Shareholders approved all ordinary resolutions, including the 2025 board work report, the independent non-executive directors’ duty performance report, and the 2025 profit distribution plan, reflecting broad support for management’s past performance and capital allocation. They also backed the re-appointment of PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor for 2026 and granted the board general mandates to repurchase and issue H shares, enhancing the company’s financial flexibility and potential tools for future capital management and strategic financing.

More about Shenzhen Creality 3D Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

Shenzhen Creality 3D Technology Co., Ltd. is a China-incorporated joint stock company listed in Hong Kong, operating in the technology and manufacturing sector with a focus on 3D printing-related products and services. Its shareholder base is dominated by several controlling shareholders and related entities, which collectively hold a majority of the company’s issued H shares and exert significant influence over corporate decisions.

Laekna, Inc. ( (HK:2105) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Laekna, Inc., a Cayman Islands-incorporated biopharmaceutical company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, focuses on innovative drug development for global markets and operates under a board structure that includes executive, non-executive and independent directors. Its capital market presence in Hong Kong underscores the importance of maintaining investor confidence and prudent balance-sheet management to support its research-driven growth strategy.

The company has announced a voluntary plan for on-market share repurchases under its existing shareholder-approved mandate, alongside open-market share purchases by its chairman and senior management, with a combined potential investment of up to HK$56 million. The buyback, to be funded from the company’s cash resources and followed by cancellation of repurchased shares, together with insider share purchases funded personally, signals management’s confidence in Laekna’s outlook and is intended to enhance shareholder value, although execution remains subject to market conditions and discretionary decisions by the board and management.

data.title: Abbisko and AstraZeneca Launch Joint Trial of Novel NSCLC Combination
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Abbisko Cayman Limited ( (HK:2256) ) has shared an announcement.

Abbisko Therapeutics has entered a strategic collaboration with AstraZeneca to conduct a multicenter Phase I/II clinical trial combining lumipodlin, a first‑in‑class oral PD‑L1 inhibitor, with AstraZeneca’s EGFR‑TKI Tagrisso in patients with EGFR‑mutated, PD‑L1 positive locally advanced or metastatic non‑small cell lung cancer. The investigational new drug application for this combination was cleared by China’s National Medical Products Administration, positioning Abbisko to lead the Phase II study and share trial responsibilities with AstraZeneca, potentially strengthening its clinical pipeline in a high‑need NSCLC segment while highlighting the promise of oral immune‑oncology agents alongside established targeted therapies.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2256) stock is a Buy
with a HK$27.10 price target.

see the HK:2256 Stock Forecast page.

More about Abbisko Cayman Limited

Abbisko Cayman Limited, through its subsidiary Abbisko Therapeutics, operates in the biopharmaceutical industry with a focus on innovative oncology therapies. The company is developing lumipodlin (ABSK043), a novel orally bioavailable small‑molecule PD‑L1 inhibitor aimed at treating non‑small cell lung cancer and addressing unmet needs in EGFR‑mutated, PD‑L1 positive NSCLC.

data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

CALB Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3931) ) has provided an update.

CALB Group has further amended its 2024–2026 Engineering and Construction Framework Agreement with Jiangsu Chengdong Construction, a connected party, by entering into a third supplemental agreement to raise the annual spending cap for 2026. The revision reflects higher-than-expected construction demand across the group’s industrial parks and facilities, indicating ongoing capacity build-out while keeping the core commercial terms of the framework unchanged.

Because Jiangsu Chengdong Construction is an associate of a substantial shareholder, the revised 2026 cap constitutes a continuing connected transaction under Hong Kong listing rules and triggers reporting, announcement and annual review requirements, but is exempt from circular and independent shareholder approval. Historical fees of over RMB380 million in 2024 and rising spending in 2025 and early 2026 underline the scale of CALB’s construction programme and the regulatory importance of monitoring related-party arrangements.

data.title: SSY Group Wins NMPA Approvals for Methocarbamol Tablets and Phloroglucinol Bulk Drug
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

SSY Group ( (HK:2005) ) has shared an update.

SSY Group has become the first entity in mainland China to secure National Medical Products Administration approval for the production and registration of 0.5g Methocarbamol Tablets, a muscle relaxant used to treat joint and muscle sprains, lumbar muscle strain and sciatica. The drug’s consistency evaluation status underscores the group’s capability to meet stringent regulatory standards in the competitive generic medicines market.

The company also obtained NMPA registration approval for Phloroglucinol Dihydrate as a bulk drug, following its earlier clearance for Phloroglucinol Injection, enabling broader use in preparations targeting acute spasmodic pain in digestive, urinary and gynecological systems. These approvals expand SSY Group’s pain-management portfolio and signal continued progress in product development, which may enhance its positioning in China’s hospital and prescription drug segments.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2005) stock is a Buy
with a HK$5.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on SSY Group stock,
see the HK:2005 Stock Forecast page.

More about SSY Group

SSY Group Limited is a pharmaceutical company based in China, focusing on the development, production and registration of chemical drugs for the domestic healthcare market. The group’s portfolio includes muscle relaxants and treatments for pain related to digestive, urinary and gynecological conditions, reflecting a strategy centered on specialty therapeutics approved by Chinese regulators.

data.title: Venus Medtech Explores Up to RMB500 Million Investment via Shares and Convertible Bonds
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Venus Medtech (Hangzhou), Inc. Class H ( (HK:2500) ) just unveiled an update.

Venus Medtech (Hangzhou) Inc. has signed a non-binding investment term sheet with Hangzhou Hi-Tech, Hangzhou Yingzhiqin and other potential investors for a prospective capital injection of up to RMB500 million. The funding would be raised through a mix of new share subscriptions and new convertible bonds, with pricing linked to the recent average trading price of the company’s Hong Kong-listed shares and capped at HK$2.5 per share.

Under the proposal, investors would subscribe for new shares at 100% of the 20-day average closing price and for new convertible bonds at 110% of that average, both subject to the price cap, potentially providing Venus Medtech with additional growth capital and balance sheet flexibility. The parties have agreed to pay RMB100 million in earnest money within seven business days, which is refundable if the transactions fail due to the company’s fault, but the deal remains subject to definitive documentation, regulatory approvals and shareholder mandate, meaning there is no assurance it will proceed and investors are urged to act cautiously.

More about Venus Medtech (Hangzhou), Inc. Class H

Venus Medtech (Hangzhou) Inc. is a China-based medical technology company focused on developing and commercializing cardiovascular implantable devices and related interventional solutions. Listed in Hong Kong, the company targets both domestic and international markets for advanced heart valve therapies and other structural heart disease treatments.

data.description:

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

HSBC Holdings ( (GB:HSBA) ) has provided an update.

HSBC Holdings has disclosed transactions involving its senior executives under UK market abuse regulations, following the automatic reinvestment of its first interim dividend for 2026 into ordinary shares. The filing notes that Co-Chief Executive for Asia and the Middle East, David Liao, acquired 4,774 shares at £14.3297 per share, and another PDMR, Barry O’Byrne, acquired 10 shares through the same dividend reinvestment mechanism.

The announcement underscores HSBC’s adherence to regulatory transparency around dealings by persons discharging managerial responsibilities, which is closely watched by investors for signals about executive alignment with shareholder interests. Such disclosures help maintain market confidence and provide insight into how senior leaders’ remuneration and incentives are tied to the bank’s equity performance, particularly in strategically important regions like Asia and the Middle East.

More about HSBC Holdings

HSBC Holdings plc is a global banking and financial services group headquartered in London, serving retail, commercial, and institutional clients across Asia, Europe, the Middle East, and the Americas. The bank focuses on universal banking, including wealth management, corporate banking, and global markets, with a strong strategic emphasis on Asia and cross-border financial flows.

Mirxes Holding Company Limited has provided a quarterly update on its efforts to resume trading in its shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, following delays in publishing its 2025 annual results and annual report. The company has established an independent investigation committee to oversee a forensic probe into prepayments and significant cash outflows, and is working with the board to align the scope of this investigation with resumption guidance issued by the exchange.

The company reports that the investigation into outstanding audit matters is ongoing and that it will provide further updates as appropriate. data.title: CA Cultural Technology Group Boosts Margins but Posts Wider Annual Loss
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

CA Cultural Technology Group Ltd. ( (HK:1566) ) has issued an update.

CA Cultural Technology Group reported revenue of HK$360.4 million for the year ended 31 March 2026, a slight 1.8% decline from the prior year, but gross profit surged 35.9% to HK$121.1 million as cost of sales fell and gross margin improved to 33.6%. The company also recorded higher other income and reduced administrative expenses, reflecting efforts to tighten operations and improve profitability at the gross level.

Despite operational improvements, the group’s loss attributable to shareholders widened sharply to HK$99.9 million, more than triple the previous year, with basic loss per share rising to 8.5 HK cents. High finance costs of over HK$106 million and an overall rise in expenses such as selling, marketing and distribution weighed heavily on the bottom line, underscoring ongoing balance sheet and funding pressures for investors and other stakeholders.

More about CA Cultural Technology Group Ltd.

CA Cultural Technology Group Ltd. operates in the cultural and technology sector, providing products and services that generate revenue primarily from sales and related services in Hong Kong and surrounding markets. The group focuses on technology-driven cultural offerings, with its performance closely tied to cost management, financing structure and overall efficiency of operations.

Nanjing Leads Biolabs has granted a total of 2,254,691 H share awards to 149 employees under its H Share Award Scheme, including executive director and CFO Mr. Zuo, with all awards satisfied by existing shares acquired on the market to avoid dilution of current shareholders. data.title: Unity Group’s Profit Surges Despite Revenue Decline on Financial Gains
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Unity Group Holdings International Limited ( (HK:1539) ) has provided an update.

Unity Group Holdings International Limited reported a 24.6% drop in revenue to HK$118.7 million for the year ended 31 March 2026, as both energy-saving system provisions and product trading declined. Despite lower sales, gross profit fell by a smaller margin, down 14.5% to HK$79.9 million, reflecting resilient margins in its core energy-efficiency offerings.

Profitability metrics improved sharply, with EBITDA more than doubling to HK$101.3 million and net profit surging 68.2% to HK$66.6 million, supported by gains from modifications of financial assets and liabilities. The group’s adjusted profit before tax excluding major extraordinary items edged up to HK$37.0 million, while basic earnings per share nearly doubled, highlighting stronger bottom-line performance even as adjusted EPS softened, signalling a profit structure increasingly influenced by one-off financial gains.

data.title: Junshi Biosciences Licenses Autoimmune Antibody Roconkibart to Fosun Wanbang for Greater China
data.description:

Shanghai Junshi Biosciences Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1877) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Shanghai Junshi Biosciences has signed a licensing agreement with Fosun Wanbang, a subsidiary of Fosun Pharma, granting it exclusive rights to develop, register and commercialize the anti-IL-17A monoclonal antibody roconkibart (JS005) in Greater China, along with co-exclusive manufacturing rights. The deal includes an upfront payment of RMB215 million, up to RMB1.125 billion in development and sales milestones, and double-digit tiered royalties, strengthening Junshi’s monetization of its pipeline while leveraging Fosun’s commercial infrastructure.

The agreement secures long-term revenue potential for Junshi through extended royalty terms tied to patent and regulatory exclusivity, underscoring confidence in roconkibart’s prospects in autoimmune diseases such as psoriasis and ankylosing spondylitis. Roconkibart has demonstrated strong phase III efficacy and safety in moderate to severe psoriasis, and its NDA for adult plaque psoriasis has been accepted by China’s National Medical Products Administration, positioning the product as a potential new biologic competitor in the autoimmune segment and enhancing Junshi’s standing in the domestic biologics market.

data.title: Shanghai Bio-Heart Wins NMPA Nod for Bioabsorbable Coronary Stent
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Shanghai Bio-Heart Biological Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2185) ) just unveiled an update.

Shanghai Bio-Heart Biological Technology has received approval from China’s National Medical Products Administration for its Bioheart bioabsorbable drug-eluting stent system, indicated for patients with ischemic heart disease caused by native coronary lesions to improve intracoronary lumen diameter. The self-developed scaffold is designed to provide temporary vessel support, elute an anti-proliferative drug to prevent restenosis, and then fully resorb, aligning with the clinical goal of vascular restoration and potentially enhancing the company’s position in the evolving coronary intervention devices market, though the firm cautions there is no guarantee of ultimate commercial success.

The Bioheart system functions similarly to a traditional drug-eluting stent while embodying a “leave nothing behind” philosophy by avoiding permanent implants once the vessel has healed. Publication of pivotal trial results in JACC: Cardiovascular Interventions underscores the technology’s clinical validation and may bolster confidence among clinicians and investors, but the company urges shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution as product development and market adoption remain subject to uncertainty.

More about Shanghai Bio-Heart Biological Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

Shanghai Bio-Heart Biological Technology Co., Ltd. is a PRC-based medical device company focused on cardiovascular interventions, developing bioresorbable scaffolds and drug-eluting stent systems for percutaneous coronary intervention. The group targets patients with coronary artery disease and ischemic heart conditions, aiming to advance vascular restoration therapies in the Chinese market.
